diff --git a/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/download.py b/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/download.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..979f04fd8ba319006aa9568809bd326c37c77d00
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/download.py
@@ -0,0 +1,222 @@
+"""Paper download tool for ArXiv MCP Server."""
+
+from dataclasses import dataclass, field
+from datetime import datetime
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
+
+from mcp.types import TextContent as types_TextContent
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.config import Settings
+
+settings = Settings()
+
+
+@dataclass
+class ConversionStatus:
+    """Track the status of a PDF-to-Markdown conversion for a single paper."""
+
+    paper_id: str
+    status: str  # 'downloading', 'converting', 'success', 'error', 'unknown'
+    started_at: datetime
+    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
+    error: Optional[str] = None
+
+
+# Global registry: paper_id -> ConversionStatus
+conversion_statuses: Dict[str, ConversionStatus] = {}
+
+
+def get_paper_path(paper_id: str, suffix: str = ".md") -> Path:
+    """Return the full filesystem path for *paper_id* with the given *suffix*.
+
+    Creates the storage directory if it does not yet exist.
+    """
+    storage_path = Path(settings.STORAGE_PATH)
+    storage_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    return storage_path / f"{paper_id}{suffix}"
+
+
+async def handle_download(arguments: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[types_TextContent]:
+    """Handle paper download and conversion requests.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    arguments : dict
+        Must contain ``paper_id`` (str).  Optional keys:
+        * ``check_status`` (bool) â€“ if True, only report current status.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    list[types.TextContent]
+        A single-element list with a JSON payload describing the status.
+    """
+    import asyncio
+    import json
+
+    import arxiv
+
+    paper_id: str = arguments["paper_id"]
+    check_status: bool = arguments.get("check_status", False)
+
+    # --- Status-only check ---------------------------------------------------
+    if check_status:
+        md_path = get_paper_path(paper_id, ".md")
+        pdf_path = get_paper_path(paper_id, ".pdf")
+
+        if paper_id in conversion_statuses:
+            cs = conversion_statuses[paper_id]
+            payload = {
+                "status": cs.status,
+                "started_at": cs.started_at.isoformat(),
+                "completed_at": cs.completed_at.isoformat() if cs.completed_at else None,
+                "error": cs.error,
+                "message": f"Paper conversion {cs.status}",
+            }
+        elif md_path.exists():
+            payload = {
+                "status": "success",
+                "message": "Paper is ready",
+                "resource_uri": f"file://{md_path.resolve()}",
+            }
+        else:
+            payload = {
+                "status": "unknown",
+                "message": "No download or conversion in progress",
+            }
+        return [types_TextContent(type="text", text=json.dumps(payload))]
+
+    # --- Check if already available ------------------------------------------
+    md_path = get_paper_path(paper_id, ".md")
+    if md_path.exists():
+        payload = {
+            "status": "success",
+            "message": "Paper already available",
+            "resource_uri": f"file://{md_path.resolve()}",
+        }
+        return [types_TextContent(type="text", text=json.dumps(payload))]
+
+    # --- Download PDF --------------------------------------------------------
+    try:
+        client = arxiv.Client(page_size=settings.BATCH_SIZE)
+        paper = next(client.results(arxiv.Search(id_list=[paper_id])))
+    except StopIteration:
+        return [
+            types_TextContent(
+                type="text",
+                text=json.dumps(
+                    {
+                        "status": "error",
+                        "message": f"Paper {paper_id} not found on arXiv",
+                    }
+                ),
+            )
+        ]
+    except Exception as exc:
+        return [
+            types_TextContent(
+                type="text",
+                text=json.dumps(
+                    {
+                        "status": "error",
+                        "message": f"Failed to fetch paper metadata: {exc}",
+                    }
+                ),
+            )
+        ]
+
+    conversion_statuses[paper_id] = ConversionStatus(
+        paper_id=paper_id,
+        status="downloading",
+        started_at=datetime.now(),
+    )
+
+    pdf_path = get_paper_path(paper_id, ".pdf")
+    try:
+        await asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(
+            None, _download_pdf_sync, paper, pdf_path
+        )
+    except Exception as exc:
+        conversion_statuses[paper_id] = ConversionStatus(
+            paper_id=paper_id,
+            status="error",
+            started_at=datetime.now(),
+            error=str(exc),
+        )
+        return [
+            types_TextContent(
+                type="text",
+                text=json.dumps(
+                    {
+                        "status": "error",
+                        "message": f"Download failed: {exc}",
+                    }
+                ),
+            )
+        ]
+
+    # --- Convert PDF -> Markdown ---------------------------------------------
+    conversion_statuses[paper_id] = ConversionStatus(
+        paper_id=paper_id,
+        status="converting",
+        started_at=conversion_statuses[paper_id].started_at,
+    )
+
+    try:
+        await asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(
+            None, _convert_pdf_to_md_sync, pdf_path, md_path
+        )
+    except Exception as exc:
+        conversion_statuses[paper_id] = ConversionStatus(
+            paper_id=paper_id,
+            status="error",
+            started_at=conversion_statuses[paper_id].started_at,
+            completed_at=datetime.now(),
+            error=str(exc),
+        )
+        return [
+            types_TextContent(
+                type="text",
+                text=json.dumps(
+                    {
+                        "status": "error",
+                        "message": f"Conversion failed: {exc}",
+                    }
+                ),
+            )
+        ]
+
+    conversion_statuses[paper_id] = ConversionStatus(
+        paper_id=paper_id,
+        status="success",
+        started_at=conversion_statuses[paper_id].started_at,
+        completed_at=datetime.now(),
+    )
+
+    payload = {
+        "status": "success",
+        "message": "Paper downloaded and converted successfully",
+        "started_at": conversion_statuses[paper_id].started_at.isoformat(),
+        "completed_at": conversion_statuses[paper_id].completed_at.isoformat(),
+        "resource_uri": f"file://{md_path.resolve()}",
+    }
+    return [types_TextContent(type="text", text=json.dumps(payload))]
+
+
+# ------------------------------------------------------------------ helpers
+
+
+def _download_pdf_sync(paper, pdf_path: Path) -> None:
+    """Synchronous helper: download a paper's PDF to *pdf_path*."""
+    import urllib.request
+
+    pdf_url = paper.pdf_url
+    urllib.request.urlretrieve(pdf_url, str(pdf_path))
+
+
+def _convert_pdf_to_md_sync(pdf_path: Path, md_path: Path) -> None:
+    """Synchronous helper: convert *pdf_path* to Markdown at *md_path*."""
+    import pymupdf4llm
+
+    md_text = pymupdf4llm.to_markdown(str(pdf_path))
+    md_path.write_text(md_text, encoding="utf-8")

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..71a6933a6b80dbe7fbd6ef6d7045575281610a2a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/list_papers.py
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+"""List papers tool for ArXiv MCP Server."""
+
+import json
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
+
+from mcp.types import TextContent as types_TextContent
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.config import settings
+
+
+def list_papers() -> List[str]:
+    """Scan local storage and return a list of paper IDs (stems of .md files)."""
+    storage_path = Path(settings.STORAGE_PATH)
+    if not storage_path.exists():
+        return []
+    return sorted([p.stem for p in storage_path.glob("*.md")])
+
+
+def _extract_paper_metadata(md_path: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+    """Extract basic metadata from a Markdown paper file.
+
+    Attempts to pull title from the first ``# heading`` line and returns
+    whatever structural hints are available.
+    """
+    meta: Dict[str, Any] = {
+        "id": md_path.stem,
+        "file_path": str(md_path.resolve()),
+        "resource_uri": f"file://{md_path.resolve()}",
+        "size_bytes": md_path.stat().st_size,
+    }
+
+    try:
+        text = md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+        # Try to grab the title from the first heading line
+        for line in text.splitlines():
+            stripped = line.strip()
+            if stripped.startswith("# "):
+                meta["title"] = stripped[2:].strip()
+                break
+            elif stripped and not stripped.startswith("#"):
+                # First non-empty, non-heading line as fallback
+                meta["first_line"] = stripped[:120]
+                break
+
+        # Count approximate page-length
+        meta["lines"] = text.count("\n") + 1
+        meta["characters"] = len(text)
+    except Exception:
+        pass
+
+    return meta
+
+
+async def handle_list_papers(arguments: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -> List[types_TextContent]:
+    """Handle requests to list all locally downloaded papers.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    arguments : dict | None
+        Currently unused; kept for MCP protocol compatibility.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    List[types.TextContent]
+        A single-element list whose ``text`` field is a JSON payload with
+        ``total_papers``, ``storage_path``, and a ``papers`` array of
+        per-paper metadata dicts.
+    """
+    paper_ids = list_papers()
+    storage_path = Path(settings.STORAGE_PATH)
+
+    papers: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
+    for pid in paper_ids:
+        md_path = storage_path / f"{pid}.md"
+        papers.append(_extract_paper_metadata(md_path))
+
+    response = {
+        "total_papers": len(papers),
+        "storage_path": str(storage_path.resolve()),
+        "papers": papers,
+    }
+
+    return [types_TextContent(type="text", text=json.dumps(response))]
diff --git a/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/read_paper.py b/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/read_paper.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..0cc6e1e831e6dc8853e86ab1c65241a705c47e9d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/read_paper.py
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+"""Read paper tool for ArXiv MCP Server."""
+
+import json
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any, Dict, List
+
+from mcp.types import TextContent as types_TextContent
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download import get_paper_path
+
+
+async def handle_read_paper(arguments: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[types_TextContent]:
+    """Read and return Markdown-formatted content of a downloaded paper.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    arguments : dict
+        Must contain ``paper_id`` (str).
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    list[types.TextContent]
+        A single-element list whose text is the Markdown content (or a JSON
+        error payload when the paper is not available).
+    """
+    paper_id: str = arguments["paper_id"]
+    md_path: Path = get_paper_path(paper_id, ".md")
+
+    if not md_path.exists():
+        return [
+            types_TextContent(
+                type="text",
+                text=json.dumps(
+                    {
+                        "status": "error",
+                        "message": f"Paper {paper_id} not found in local storage. "
+                                   "Download it first with the download_paper tool.",
+                    }
+                ),
+            )
+        ]
+
+    try:
+        content = md_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    except OSError as exc:
+        return [
+            types_TextContent(
+                type="text",
+                text=json.dumps(
+                    {
+                        "status": "error",
+                        "message": f"Failed to read paper: {exc}",
+                    }
+                ),
+            )
+        ]
+
+    return [
+        types_TextContent(
+            type="text",
+            text=json.dumps(
+                {
+                    "paper_id": paper_id,
+                    "resource_uri": f"file://{md_path.resolve()}",
+                    "content": content,
+                }
+            ),
+        )
+    ]
diff --git a/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/search.py b/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/search.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..2a23ff9927f14c3831db24e8843ca20c56632604
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/arxiv_mcp_server/tools/search.py
@@ -0,0 +1,156 @@
+"""Search tool for ArXiv MCP Server."""
+
+import json
+from datetime import datetime
+from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
+
+import arxiv
+from mcp.types import TextContent as types_TextContent
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.config import settings
+
+
+# Field specifiers supported by arXiv
+FIELD_SPECIFIERS = {"all:", "ti:", "abs:", "au:", "cat:", "co:", "r:", "j:", "rn:", "yr:", "id:", "or:"}
+
+
+def _has_field_specifier(query: str) -> bool:
+    """Check if the query already contains a field specifier."""
+    for specifier in FIELD_SPECIFIERS:
+        if query.startswith(specifier):
+            return True
+    return False
+
+
+def _build_query(query: str, categories: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> str:
+    """Build the arXiv search query with field specifiers and optional category filter.
+
+    If the query does not already start with a field specifier, wrap it with `all:`
+    so that arXiv searches across all fields, improving relevance.  If categories
+    are provided, append a `cat:` filter for each category.
+    """
+    # If no field specifier is present, default to searching all fields
+    if not _has_field_specifier(query):
+        query = f"all:{query}"
+
+    parts = [query]
+    if categories:
+        for cat in categories:
+            parts.append(f"cat:{cat}")
+
+    return " AND ".join(parts)
+
+
+def _validate_date(date_str: str, field_name: str) -> str:
+    """Validate and normalize a date string to 'YYYYMMDD' format expected by arxiv API.
+
+    Raises ValueError with a descriptive message on invalid input.
+    """
+    for fmt in ("%Y-%m-%d", "%Y%m%d", "%Y/%m/%d"):
+        try:
+            return datetime.strptime(date_str, fmt).strftime("%Y%m%d")
+        except ValueError:
+            continue
+    raise ValueError(
+        f"Invalid date format for '{field_name}': '{date_str}'. "
+        f"Use ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)."
+    )
+
+
+def _format_paper(paper: arxiv.Result) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+    """Convert an arxiv.Result into a plain dict for JSON serialization."""
+    return {
+        "id": paper.entry_id.split("/")[-1],
+        "title": paper.title,
+        "authors": paper.authors,
+        "abstract": paper.summary,
+        "categories": list(paper.categories),
+        "published": paper.published.isoformat() if paper.published else None,
+        "url": paper.pdf_url,
+        "resource_uri": f"arxiv://{paper.entry_id.split('/')[-1]}",
+    }
+
+
+async def handle_search(arguments: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[types_TextContent]:
+    """Handle paper search requests.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    arguments : dict
+        - query (str): The search query string.
+        - max_results (int, optional): Maximum number of results (default: 10).
+        - date_from (str, optional): Start date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).
+        - date_to (str, optional): End date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).
+        - categories (List[str], optional): Category filters (e.g. ["cs.AI", "cs.LG"]).
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    List[types.TextContent]
+        A single-element list whose ``text`` field contains the JSON-serialised
+        search results.
+    """
+    query: str = arguments.get("query", "")
+    max_results: int = arguments.get("max_results", 10)
+    date_from: Optional[str] = arguments.get("date_from")
+    date_to: Optional[str] = arguments.get("date_to")
+    categories: Optional[List[str]] = arguments.get("categories")
+
+    # Clamp max_results to configured limit
+    if max_results > settings.MAX_RESULTS:
+        max_results = settings.MAX_RESULTS
+
+    # --- Date validation ---------------------------------------------------
+    if date_from:
+        try:
+            date_from = _validate_date(date_from, "date_from")
+        except ValueError as exc:
+            return [
+                types_TextContent(
+                    type="text",
+                    text=f"Error: Invalid date format for date_from: {exc.args[0]}",
+                )
+            ]
+
+    if date_to:
+        try:
+            date_to = _validate_date(date_to, "date_to")
+        except ValueError as exc:
+            return [
+                types_TextContent(
+                    type="text",
+                    text=f"Error: Invalid date format for date_to: {exc.args[0]}",
+                )
+            ]
+
+    # --- Build the search query --------------------------------------------
+    search_query = _build_query(query, categories)
+
+    # --- Execute the arXiv search ------------------------------------------
+    try:
+        search = arxiv.Search(
+            query=search_query,
+            max_results=max_results,
+            sort_by=arxiv.SortCriterion.Relevance,
+            sort_order=arxiv.SortOrder.Descending,
+        )
+
+        client = arxiv.Client(page_size=settings.BATCH_SIZE)
+        results = list(client.results(search))
+    except Exception as exc:
+        return [
+            types_TextContent(
+                type="text",
+                text=json.dumps({"error": f"Search failed: {str(exc)}"}),
+            )
+        ]
+
+    # --- Format results ----------------------------------------------------
+    papers = [_format_paper(p) for p in results]
+
+    response = {
+        "total_results": len(papers),
+        "query": query,
+        "papers": papers,
+    }
+
+    return [types_TextContent(type="text", text=json.dumps(response))]

diff --git a/tests/test_config.py b/tests/test_config.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..95479ff773c92167755af2e7857644cb22771379
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_config.py
@@ -0,0 +1,117 @@
+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.config â€” Settings, STORAGE_PATH, CLI args."""
+
+import sys
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import patch
+
+import pytest
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.config import Settings
+
+
+class TestSettingsDefaults:
+    """Test default attribute values on Settings."""
+
+    def test_app_name(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.APP_NAME == "arxiv-mcp-server"
+
+    def test_app_version(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.APP_VERSION == "0.2.11"
+
+    def test_max_results(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.MAX_RESULTS == 50
+
+    def test_batch_size(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.BATCH_SIZE == 20
+
+    def test_request_timeout(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.REQUEST_TIMEOUT == 60
+
+    def test_host(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.HOST == "0.0.0.0"
+
+    def test_port(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.PORT == 8000
+
+
+class TestStoragePathDefault:
+    """Test STORAGE_PATH fallback behaviour."""
+
+    def test_storage_path_is_home_based(self):
+        """Without CLI args the path must live under ~/.arxiv-mcp-server/papers."""
+        with patch.object(sys, "argv", ["program"]):
+            s = Settings()
+            expected = (Path.home() / ".arxiv-mcp-server" / "papers").resolve()
+            assert s.STORAGE_PATH == expected
+
+    def test_storage_path_creates_directory(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        """STORAGE_PATH should mkdir the directory if missing."""
+        new_dir = tmp_path / "sub" / "papers"
+        with patch.object(sys, "argv", ["program", "--storage-path", str(new_dir)]):
+            s = Settings()
+            assert s.STORAGE_PATH == new_dir.resolve()
+            assert s.STORAGE_PATH.exists()
+
+
+class TestStoragePathFromArgs:
+    """Test _get_storage_path_from_args CLI parsing."""
+
+    def test_simple(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        p = s._get_storage_path_from_args()
+        assert p is None  # no --storage-path in sys.argv
+
+    def test_from_argv(self):
+        test_path = "/tmp/test_storage"
+        with patch.object(sys, "argv", ["program", "--storage-path", test_path]):
+            s = Settings()
+            assert s._get_storage_path_from_args() == Path(test_path)
+
+    def test_first_match_wins(self):
+        with patch.object(sys, "argv", ["program", "--storage-path", "/first", "--storage-path", "/second"]):
+            s = Settings()
+            assert s._get_storage_path_from_args() == Path("/first")
+
+    def test_missing_value_returns_none(self):
+        """--storage-path at the very end (no value) â†’ None."""
+        with patch.object(sys, "argv", ["program", "--storage-path"]):
+            s = Settings()
+            assert s._get_storage_path_from_args() is None
+
+
+class TestPlatformCompatibility:
+    """Cross-platform path normalisation."""
+
+    def test_unix_path(self, tmp_path):
+        unix = str(tmp_path / "papers")
+        with patch.object(sys, "argv", ["program", "--storage-path", unix]):
+            s = Settings()
+            assert s.STORAGE_PATH == Path(unix).resolve()
+
+    def test_path_with_spaces(self, tmp_path):
+        sp_dir = tmp_path / "path with spaces" / "papers"
+        with patch.object(sys, "argv", ["program", "--storage-path", str(sp_dir)]):
+            s = Settings()
+            assert s.STORAGE_PATH == sp_dir.resolve()
+            assert s.STORAGE_PATH.exists()
+
+
+class TestSettingsIsPydantic:
+    """Settings must inherit from BaseSettings."""
+
+    def test_is_pydantic_base(self):
+        from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
+        assert issubclass(Settings, BaseSettings)
+
+    def test_model_dump(self):
+        s = Settings()
+        d = s.model_dump()
+        assert d["APP_NAME"] == "arxiv-mcp-server"
+        assert d["PORT"] == 8000
diff --git a/tests/test_download.py b/tests/test_download.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..3d7617f6615642825f09104a3ca895431db3b0fb
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_download.py
@@ -0,0 +1,215 @@
+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download â€” handle_download, get_paper_path, ConversionStatus."""
+
+import json
+from dataclasses import is_dataclass
+from datetime import datetime
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download import (
+    ConversionStatus,
+    conversion_statuses,
+    get_paper_path,
+    handle_download,
+)
+
+
+class TestConversionStatus:
+    def test_is_dataclass(self):
+        assert is_dataclass(ConversionStatus)
+
+    def test_required_fields(self):
+        cs = ConversionStatus(
+            paper_id="1234.56789",
+            status="downloading",
+            started_at=datetime(2024, 1, 1),
+        )
+        assert cs.paper_id == "1234.56789"
+        assert cs.status == "downloading"
+        assert cs.completed_at is None
+        assert cs.error is None
+
+    def test_optional_fields(self):
+        cs = ConversionStatus(
+            paper_id="1234.56789",
+            status="error",
+            started_at=datetime(2024, 1, 1),
+            completed_at=datetime(2024, 1, 2),
+            error="timeout",
+        )
+        assert cs.completed_at == datetime(2024, 1, 2)
+        assert cs.error == "timeout"
+
+
+class TestConversionStatusesDict:
+    def test_is_dict(self):
+        assert isinstance(conversion_statuses, dict)
+
+
+class TestGetPaperPath:
+    def test_default_suffix(self):
+        path = get_paper_path("2103.12345")
+        assert path.name == "2103.12345.md"
+        assert path.suffix == ".md"
+
+    def test_explicit_pdf_suffix(self):
+        path = get_paper_path("2103.12345", ".pdf")
+        assert path.name == "2103.12345.pdf"
+        assert path.suffix == ".pdf"
+
+    def test_returns_path_obj(self):
+        assert isinstance(get_paper_path("x"), Path)
+
+    def test_in_storage_dir(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server.config import settings
+        path = get_paper_path("x")
+        assert str(path.parent) == str(settings.STORAGE_PATH)
+
+
+class TestHandleDownloadExisting:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_existing_md_returns_success(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        md_file = tmp_path / "test.md"
+        md_file.write_text("# Test\nContent", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "test"})
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["status"] == "success"
+        assert "already available" in data["message"]
+        assert "resource_uri" in data
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_existing_paper_via_check_status(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        md_file = tmp_path / "test.md"
+        md_file.write_text("# Test\nContent", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "test", "check_status": True})
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["status"] == "success"
+        assert "ready" in data["message"].lower()
+
+
+class TestHandleDownloadNonexistent:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_paper_not_found(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_client = MagicMock()
+        mock_client.results.return_value = iter([])
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        with patch("arxiv.Client", return_value=mock_client):
+            result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "invalid.12345"})
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["status"] == "error"
+            assert "not found on arXiv" in data["message"]
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_arxiv_client_raises(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        with patch("arxiv.Client", side_effect=Exception("network error")):
+            result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "2103.12345"})
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["status"] == "error"
+            assert "Failed to fetch paper metadata" in data["message"]
+
+
+class TestHandleDownloadCheckStatus:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_unknown_status(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        conversion_statuses.clear()
+        result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "no-such.12345", "check_status": True})
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["status"] == "unknown"
+        assert "No download or conversion" in data["message"]
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_in_progress_status(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        conversion_statuses.clear()
+        conversion_statuses["test.123"] = ConversionStatus(
+            paper_id="test.123",
+            status="converting",
+            started_at=datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0, 0),
+        )
+        result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "test.123", "check_status": True})
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["status"] == "converting"
+        assert data["started_at"] == "2024-06-01T10:00:00"
+        del conversion_statuses["test.123"]
+
+
+class TestHandleDownloadFullLifecycle:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_successful_download_and_convert(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_client = MagicMock()
+        mock_paper = MagicMock()
+        mock_paper.pdf_url = "https://arxiv.org/pdf/test.123.pdf"
+        mock_client.results.return_value = iter([mock_paper])
+
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+
+        mocker.patch("urllib.request.urlretrieve")
+        mocker.patch("pymupdf4llm.to_markdown", return_value="# Converted\nBody")
+
+        with patch("arxiv.Client", return_value=mock_client):
+            result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "test.123"})
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["status"] == "success"
+            assert "downloaded and converted" in data["message"]
+            assert "resource_uri" in data
+            assert (tmp_path / "test.123.md").exists()
+            assert (tmp_path / "test.123.md").read_text().startswith("# Converted")
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_download_failure(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_client = MagicMock()
+        mock_paper = MagicMock()
+        mock_paper.pdf_url = "https://arxiv.org/pdf/test.123.pdf"
+        mock_client.results.return_value = iter([mock_paper])
+
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+
+        mocker.patch("urllib.request.urlretrieve", side_effect=OSError("disk full"))
+
+        with patch("arxiv.Client", return_value=mock_client):
+            result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "test.123"})
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["status"] == "error"
+            assert "Download failed" in data["message"]
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_conversion_failure(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_client = MagicMock()
+        mock_paper = MagicMock()
+        mock_paper.pdf_url = "https://arxiv.org/pdf/test.123.pdf"
+        mock_client.results.return_value = iter([mock_paper])
+
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+
+        mocker.patch("urllib.request.urlretrieve")
+        mocker.patch("pymupdf4llm.to_markdown", side_effect=ValueError("bad pdf"))
+
+        with patch("arxiv.Client", return_value=mock_client):
+            result = await handle_download({"paper_id": "test.123"})
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["status"] == "error"
+            assert "Conversion failed" in data["message"]

+"""Tests for src/arxiv_mcp_server/__init__.py â€” unified API entry point."""
+
+import pytest
+
+
+class TestVersionInfo:
+    def test_version_string(self):
+        import arxiv_mcp_server
+        assert arxiv_mcp_server.__version__ == "0.2.11"
+
+    def test_version_in_all_not_required(self):
+        import arxiv_mcp_server
+        assert hasattr(arxiv_mcp_server, "__version__")
+
+
+class TestModuleExports:
+    def test_list_prompts_exported(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server import list_prompts
+        assert callable(list_prompts)
+
+    def test_get_prompt_exported(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server import get_prompt
+        assert callable(get_prompt)
+
+    def test_handle_download_exported(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server import handle_download
+        assert callable(handle_download)
+
+    def test_get_paper_path_exported(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server import get_paper_path
+        assert callable(get_paper_path)
+
+    def test_conversion_statuses_exported(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server import conversion_statuses
+        assert isinstance(conversion_statuses, dict)
+
+    def test_handle_search_exported(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server import handle_search
+        assert callable(handle_search)
+
+    def test_settings_exported(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server import Settings
+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.APP_NAME == "arxiv-mcp-server"
+        assert hasattr(s, "STORAGE_PATH")
+
+    def test_all_list(self):
+        import arxiv_mcp_server
+        required = {
+            "list_prompts",
+            "get_prompt",
+            "handle_download",
+            "get_paper_path",
+            "conversion_statuses",
+            "handle_search",
+            "Settings",
+        }
+        assert required.issubset(set(arxiv_mcp_server.__all__))
+
+
+class TestDirectImports:
+    def test_import_from_tools(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server.tools import handle_search, handle_download
+        assert callable(handle_search)
+        assert callable(handle_download)
+
+    def test_import_from_prompts_handlers(self):
+        from arxiv_mcp_server.prompts.handlers import list_prompts, get_prompt
+        assert callable(list_prompts)

+    def test_import_from_tools_download(self):

+        assert isinstance(conversion_statuses, dict)
+

+        s = Settings()
+        assert s.APP_NAME == "arxiv-mcp-server"

+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers â€” handle_list_papers, list_papers."""
+
+import json
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import patch
+
+import pytest
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers import (
+    handle_list_papers,
+    list_papers,
+    _extract_paper_metadata,
+)
+
+
+class TestListPapers:
+    """Test list_papers() scanning."""
+
+    def test_empty_storage(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        assert list_papers() == []
+
+    def test_single_paper(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        (tmp_path / "abc.md").write_text("# Title\nContent", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        ids = list_papers()
+        assert ids == ["abc"]
+
+    def test_multiple_papers_sorted(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        (tmp_path / "zzz.md").write_text("z", encoding="utf-8")
+        (tmp_path / "aaa.md").write_text("a", encoding="utf-8")
+        (tmp_path / "mmm.md").write_text("m", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        ids = list_papers()
+        assert ids == ["aaa", "mmm", "zzz"]
+
+    def test_ignores_non_md_files(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        (tmp_path / "abc.md").write_text("a", encoding="utf-8")
+        (tmp_path / "data.txt").write_text("x", encoding="utf-8")
+        (tmp_path / "notes.pdf").write_text("x", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        assert list_papers() == ["abc"]
+
+
+class TestExtractPaperMetadata:
+    def test_basic_metadata(self, tmp_path):
+        md_file = tmp_path / "1234.md"
+        md_file.write_text("# My Paper\n\nSome content here.\nMore text.\n", encoding="utf-8")
+        meta = _extract_paper_metadata(md_file)
+        assert meta["id"] == "1234"
+        assert meta["title"] == "My Paper"
+        assert meta["resource_uri"] == f"file://{md_file.resolve()}"
+        assert meta["size_bytes"] > 0
+
+    def test_no_heading_uses_first_line(self, tmp_path):
+        md_file = tmp_path / "1234.md"
+        md_file.write_text("Just plain text\nNo heading.\n", encoding="utf-8")
+        meta = _extract_paper_metadata(md_file)
+        assert "title" not in meta
+        assert meta.get("first_line") == "Just plain text"
+
+    def test_counts_lines_and_chars(self, tmp_path):
+        md_file = tmp_path / "1234.md"
+        text = "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
+        md_file.write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")
+        meta = _extract_paper_metadata(md_file)
+        assert meta["lines"] == 4  # 3 lines + trailing newline = 4 lines
+        assert meta["characters"] == len(text)
+
+    def test_nonexistent_file_does_not_crash(self, tmp_path):
+        md_file = tmp_path / "missing.md"
+        with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
+            _extract_paper_metadata(md_file)
+
+
+class TestHandleListPapers:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_empty(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        result = await handle_list_papers()
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["total_papers"] == 0
+        assert data["papers"] == []
+        assert "storage_path" in data
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_with_papers(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        (tmp_path / "paper1.md").write_text("# Paper One\nContent", encoding="utf-8")
+        (tmp_path / "paper2.md").write_text("# Paper Two\nMore content", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        result = await handle_list_papers()
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["total_papers"] == 2
+        assert len(data["papers"]) == 2
+        assert data["papers"][0]["id"] == "paper1"
+        assert data["papers"][1]["id"] == "paper2"
+        assert data["papers"][0]["title"] == "Paper One"
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_with_none_arguments(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.list_papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        result = await handle_list_papers(None)
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["total_papers"] == 0

+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.prompts â€” handlers, prompt_manager, PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT."""
+
+import pytest
+
+from mcp.types import GetPromptResult, Prompt, PromptMessage, TextContent
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.prompts.handlers import list_prompts, get_prompt
+from arxiv_mcp_server.prompts.prompt_manager import PromptManager, prompt_manager
+from arxiv_mcp_server.prompts.deep_research_analysis_prompt import (
+    PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT,
+)
+
+
+class TestPaperAnalysisPrompt:
+    def test_is_nonempty_string(self):
+        assert isinstance(PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT, str)
+        assert len(PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT) > 100
+
+    def test_contains_workflow_tags(self):
+        assert "<workflow-for-paper-analysis>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<preparation>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<comprehensive-analysis>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<methodology-analysis>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<results-analysis>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<practical-implications>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<theoretical-implications>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<future-directions>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<broader-impact>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "<keep-in-mind>" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+
+    def test_contains_tool_descriptions(self):
+        assert "read_paper" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "download_paper" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "search_papers" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "list_papers" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+
+    def test_contains_analysis_sections(self):
+        assert "Executive Summary" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        # Check research context via tag or content
+        assert "research-context" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "methodology-analysis" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+        assert "results-analysis" in PAPER_ANALYSIS_PROMPT
+
+
+class TestPromptManager:
+    def test_register_and_list(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        assert pm.list_prompts() == []
+        pm.register(
+            name="test",
+            description="A test prompt",
+            render=lambda args: f"Hello {args['name']}",
+        )
+        prompts = pm.list_prompts()
+        assert len(prompts) == 1
+        assert prompts[0].name == "test"
+
+    def test_get_required_args(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        pm.register(
+            name="t",
+            description="d",
+            render=lambda a: a["x"],
+            required_args=["x"],
+        )
+        assert pm.get_required_args("t") == ["x"]
+        assert pm.get_required_args("missing") == []
+
+    def test_render(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        pm.register(
+            name="greet",
+            description="Greets someone",
+            render=lambda a: f"Hi {a['name']}",
+        )
+        assert pm.render("greet", {"name": "World"}) == "Hi World"
+
+    def test_render_unknown_raises(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Prompt not found"):
+            pm.render("nope", {})
+
+    def test_has_prompt(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        assert pm.has_prompt("x") is False
+        pm.register(name="x", description="d", render=lambda a: "ok")
+        assert pm.has_prompt("x") is True
+
+    def test_prompt_names_sorted(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        pm.register(name="b", description="d", render=lambda a: "b")
+        pm.register(name="a", description="d", render=lambda a: "a")
+        assert pm.prompt_names == ["a", "b"]
+
+    def test_get_prompt_definition(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        pm.register(name="x", description="desc", render=lambda a: "x")
+        p = pm.get_prompt_definition("x")
+        assert isinstance(p, Prompt)
+        assert p.name == "x"
+        assert p.description == "desc"
+
+    def test_get_prompt_definition_missing(self):
+        pm = PromptManager()
+        assert pm.get_prompt_definition("nope") is None
+
+
+class TestBuiltinPrompts:
+    def test_deep_paper_analysis_registered(self):
+        assert prompt_manager.has_prompt("deep-paper-analysis")
+
+    def test_list_prompts_returns_deep_paper_analysis(self):
+        prompts = prompt_manager.list_prompts()
+        names = [p.name for p in prompts]
+        assert "deep-paper-analysis" in names
+
+    def test_deep_paper_analysis_has_required_args(self):
+        assert prompt_manager.get_required_args("deep-paper-analysis") == ["paper_id"]
+
+
+class TestListPromptsHandler:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_returns_list(self):
+        result = await list_prompts()
+        assert isinstance(result, list)
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_contains_deep_paper_analysis(self):
+        result = await list_prompts()
+        names = [p.name for p in result]
+        assert "deep-paper-analysis" in names
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_prompt_objects_have_description(self):
+        result = await list_prompts()
+        for p in result:
+            assert isinstance(p, Prompt)
+            assert p.description is not None and len(p.description) > 0
+
+
+class TestGetPromptHandler:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_valid_prompt(self):
+        result = await get_prompt("deep-paper-analysis", {"paper_id": "2401.00123"})
+        assert isinstance(result, GetPromptResult)
+        assert len(result.messages) == 1
+        msg = result.messages[0]
+        assert msg.role == "user"
+        assert isinstance(msg.content, TextContent)
+        assert "2401.00123" in msg.content.text
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_invalid_name_raises(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Prompt not found"):
+            await get_prompt("invalid-prompt", {"paper_id": "x"})
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_none_arguments_raises(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="No arguments provided"):
+            await get_prompt("deep-paper-analysis", None)
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_empty_arguments_raises(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="No arguments provided"):
+            await get_prompt("deep-paper-analysis", {})
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_missing_required_argument_raises(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Missing required argument"):
+            await get_prompt("deep-paper-analysis", {"other_arg": "value"})
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_result_has_description(self):
+        result = await get_prompt("deep-paper-analysis", {"paper_id": "x"})
+        assert result.description is not None
+        assert len(result.description) > 0
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_session_id_accepted(self):
+        result = await get_prompt(
+            "deep-paper-analysis",
+            {"paper_id": "2401.00123"},
+            session_id="sess-123",
+        )
+        assert isinstance(result, GetPromptResult)
+        assert "2401.00123" in result.messages[0].content.text

+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.tools.read_paper â€” handle_read_paper."""
+
+import json
+
+import pytest
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.tools.read_paper import handle_read_paper
+
+
+class TestHandleReadPaper:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_read_existing_paper(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        """Reading a paper that exists in storage returns its content."""
+        content = "# Test Paper\n\nThis is the body."
+        (tmp_path / "test.md").write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        result = await handle_read_paper({"paper_id": "test"})
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["paper_id"] == "test"
+        assert data["content"] == content
+        assert "resource_uri" in data
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_read_nonexistent_paper(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        """Reading a paper not in storage returns an error."""
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        result = await handle_read_paper({"paper_id": "nope"})
+        data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+        assert data["status"] == "error"
+        assert "not found" in data["message"]
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_read_returns_single_text_content(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        """The result list must have exactly one TextContent item."""
+        (tmp_path / "ok.md").write_text("content", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.tools.download.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        mock_settings.BATCH_SIZE = 10
+        result = await handle_read_paper({"paper_id": "ok"})
+        assert len(result) == 1
+        assert result[0].type == "text"

+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers â€” list_papers, read_paper_resource."""
+
+import pytest
+
+from mcp.server.lowlevel.helper_types import ReadResourceContents
+from mcp.types import Resource
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers import list_papers, read_paper_resource
+
+
+class TestResourceListPapers:
+    def test_empty_storage(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        assert list_papers() == []
+
+    def test_returns_resource_objects(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        (tmp_path / "abc.md").write_text("# My Paper\nBody", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        res = list_papers()
+        assert len(res) == 1
+        r = res[0]
+        assert isinstance(r, Resource)
+        assert str(r.uri) == "arxiv://abc"
+        assert r.name == "abc"
+        assert r.title == "My Paper"
+        assert r.mimeType == "text/markdown"
+
+    def test_multiple_papers_sorted(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        (tmp_path / "ccc.md").write_text("# C", encoding="utf-8")
+        (tmp_path / "aaa.md").write_text("# A", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        res = list_papers()
+        assert len(res) == 2
+        assert res[0].name == "aaa"
+        assert res[1].name == "ccc"
+
+    def test_paper_without_heading_uses_first_line_or_id(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        """When there is no # heading, the first non-empty line becomes the title.
+        If the file is completely empty, the id is used as fallback."""
+        (tmp_path / "noid.md").write_text("Just plain text", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        res = list_papers()
+        # The code takes the first line if it's not a heading
+        assert res[0].title == "Just plain text"
+
+    def test_paper_with_empty_file_uses_id(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        """Empty .md file -> title falls back to the paper ID."""
+        (tmp_path / "empty.md").write_text("", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        res = list_papers()
+        assert res[0].title == "empty"
+
+
+class TestResourceReadPaper:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_valid_uri(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        content = "# Paper\n\nBody text."
+        (tmp_path / "test.md").write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        result = await read_paper_resource("arxiv://test")
+        items = list(result)
+        assert len(items) == 1
+        item = items[0]
+        assert isinstance(item, ReadResourceContents)
+        assert item.content == content
+        assert item.mime_type == "text/markdown"
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_invalid_uri_scheme(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Unsupported resource URI scheme"):
+            await read_paper_resource("http://example.com")
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_empty_paper_id(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Empty paper_id"):
+            await read_paper_resource("arxiv://")
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_missing_paper(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError, match="not found in local storage"):
+            await read_paper_resource("arxiv://nope")
diff --git a/tests/test_search.py b/tests/test_search.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..255b44a2e61467819d27f416fcb74b5a171605c2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/test_search.py
@@ -0,0 +1,175 @@
+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.tools.search â€” handle_search."""
+
+import json
+from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.tools.search import (
+    _build_query,
+    _has_field_specifier,
+    _validate_date,
+    _format_paper,
+    handle_search,
+)
+
+
+def _make_mock_result(
+    entry_id="http://arxiv.org/abs/2103.12345v1",
+    title="Test Paper",
+    authors=["Alice Smith"],
+    summary="Abstract text",
+    categories=["cs.AI"],
+    published=None,
+    pdf_url="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2103.12345.pdf",
+):
+    m = MagicMock()
+    m.entry_id = entry_id
+    m.title = title
+    m.authors = authors
+    m.summary = summary
+    m.categories = categories
+    m.published = published
+    m.pdf_url = pdf_url
+    return m
+
+
+class TestHasFieldSpecifier:
+    def test_plain_query(self):
+        assert _has_field_specifier("machine learning") is False
+
+    def test_ti_prefix(self):
+        assert _has_field_specifier("ti:machine learning") is True
+
+    def test_abs_prefix(self):
+        assert _has_field_specifier("abs:neural networks") is True
+
+    def test_au_prefix(self):
+        assert _has_field_specifier("au:Hinton") is True
+
+    def test_cat_prefix(self):
+        assert _has_field_specifier("cat:cs.AI") is True
+
+    def test_all_prefix(self):
+        assert _has_field_specifier("all:transformer") is True
+
+
+class TestBuildQuery:
+    def test_wraps_plain_query(self):
+        assert _build_query("machine learning") == "all:machine learning"
+
+    def test_keeps_existing_specifier(self):
+        assert _build_query("ti:transformer") == "ti:transformer"
+
+    def test_appends_single_category(self):
+        q = _build_query("nlp", categories=["cs.AI"])
+        assert q == "all:nlp AND cat:cs.AI"
+
+    def test_appends_multiple_categories(self):
+        q = _build_query("nlp", categories=["cs.AI", "cs.LG"])
+        assert q == "all:nlp AND cat:cs.AI AND cat:cs.LG"
+
+    def test_categories_only(self):
+        q = _build_query("x", categories=["cs.AI"])
+        assert q == "all:x AND cat:cs.AI"
+
+
+class TestValidateDate:
+    def test_iso_dash(self):
+        assert _validate_date("2023-01-15", "d") == "20230115"
+
+    def test_compact(self):
+        assert _validate_date("20230115", "d") == "20230115"
+
+    def test_slash(self):
+        assert _validate_date("2023/01/15", "d") == "20230115"
+
+    def test_invalid(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Invalid date format"):
+            _validate_date("not-a-date", "d")
+
+    def test_includes_field_name_in_error(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="date_from"):
+            _validate_date("xyz", "date_from")
+
+
+class TestFormatPaper:
+    def test_full_format(self):
+        from datetime import datetime
+        paper = _make_mock_result(published=datetime(2021, 3, 15))
+        d = _format_paper(paper)
+        assert d["id"] == "2103.12345v1"
+        assert d["title"] == "Test Paper"
+        assert d["authors"] == ["Alice Smith"]
+        assert d["abstract"] == "Abstract text"
+        assert d["categories"] == ["cs.AI"]
+        assert d["url"] == "https://arxiv.org/pdf/2103.12345.pdf"
+        assert d["resource_uri"] == "arxiv://2103.12345v1"
+
+    def test_published_none(self):
+        paper = _make_mock_result(published=None)
+        assert _format_paper(paper)["published"] is None
+
+
+class TestHandleSearch:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_basic_search_returns_papers(self):
+        mock_paper = _make_mock_result()
+        with patch("arxiv.Client") as MockClient:
+            mock_client = MagicMock()
+            MockClient.return_value = mock_client
+            mock_client.results.return_value = [mock_paper]
+            result = await handle_search({"query": "test", "max_results": 5})
+            assert len(result) == 1
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["total_results"] == 1
+            assert data["query"] == "test"
+            assert len(data["papers"]) == 1
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_search_clamps_max_results(self):
+        with patch("arxiv.Client") as MockClient, \
+             patch("arxiv.Search") as MockSearch:
+            mock_client = MagicMock()
+            MockClient.return_value = mock_client
+            mock_client.results.return_value = []
+            await handle_search({"query": "x", "max_results": 999})
+            MockSearch.assert_called_once()
+            call_kwargs = MockSearch.call_args
+            mr = call_kwargs.kwargs.get("max_results", call_kwargs[1].get("max_results"))
+            assert mr == 50
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_invalid_date_from(self):
+        result = await handle_search({
+            "query": "test",
+            "date_from": "bad-date",
+        })
+        assert "Error: Invalid date format" in result[0].text
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_invalid_date_to(self):
+        result = await handle_search({
+            "query": "test",
+            "date_to": "bad-date",
+        })
+        assert "Error: Invalid date format" in result[0].text
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_search_error(self):
+        with patch("arxiv.Client") as MockClient:
+            MockClient.side_effect = Exception("network fail")
+            result = await handle_search({"query": "fail"})
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["error"] == "Search failed: network fail"
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_empty_results(self):
+        with patch("arxiv.Client") as MockClient:
+            mock_client = MagicMock()
+            MockClient.return_value = mock_client
+            mock_client.results.return_value = []
+            result = await handle_search({"query": "nothing"})
+            data = json.loads(result[0].text)
+            assert data["total_results"] == 0
+            assert data["papers"] == []

+"""Tests for arxiv_mcp_server.server â€” MCP server registration and handlers."""
+
+import json
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
+
+import pytest
+
+from mcp.types import Tool
+
+from arxiv_mcp_server.server import (
+    server,
+    TOOL_REGISTRY,
+    search_tool,
+    download_tool,
+    list_tool,
+    read_tool,
+    list_tools,
+    call_tool,
+    handle_list_prompts,
+    handle_get_prompt,
+    handle_list_resources,
+    handle_read_resource,
+)
+
+
+class TestToolDefinitions:
+    def test_tool_registry_has_four_tools(self):
+        assert len(TOOL_REGISTRY) == 4
+
+    def test_tool_registry_keys(self):
+        expected = {"search_papers", "download_paper", "list_papers", "read_paper"}
+        assert set(TOOL_REGISTRY.keys()) == expected
+
+    def test_search_tool_schema(self):
+        assert search_tool.name == "search_papers"
+        assert "query" in search_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+        assert "query" in search_tool.inputSchema["required"]
+        assert "max_results" in search_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+        assert "date_from" in search_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+        assert "date_to" in search_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+        assert "categories" in search_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+
+    def test_download_tool_schema(self):
+        assert download_tool.name == "download_paper"
+        assert "paper_id" in download_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+        assert "paper_id" in download_tool.inputSchema["required"]
+        assert "check_status" in download_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+
+    def test_list_tool_schema(self):
+        assert list_tool.name == "list_papers"
+
+    def test_read_tool_schema(self):
+        assert read_tool.name == "read_paper"
+        assert "paper_id" in read_tool.inputSchema["properties"]
+        assert "paper_id" in read_tool.inputSchema["required"]
+
+    def test_all_tools_are_Tool_instances(self):
+        for t in TOOL_REGISTRY.values():
+            assert isinstance(t, Tool)
+
+    def test_tools_have_descriptions(self):
+        for t in TOOL_REGISTRY.values():
+            assert t.description is not None
+            assert len(t.description) > 0
+
+
+class TestListToolsHandler:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_returns_all_tools(self):
+        tools = await list_tools()
+        assert len(tools) == 4
+        names = [t.name for t in tools]
+        assert "search_papers" in names
+        assert "download_paper" in names
+        assert "list_papers" in names
+        assert "read_paper" in names
+
+
+class TestCallToolHandler:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_unknown_tool_returns_error(self):
+        result = await call_tool("nonexistent_tool", {})
+        assert len(result) == 1
+        assert "Unknown tool" in result[0].text
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_search_papers_dispatches(self):
+        with patch("arxiv_mcp_server.server.handle_search", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_search:
+            mock_search.return_value = [MagicMock(type="text", text='{"total_results":0}')]
+            result = await call_tool("search_papers", {"query": "test"})
+            mock_search.assert_awaited_once()
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_download_paper_dispatches(self):
+        with patch("arxiv_mcp_server.server.handle_download", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_dl:
+            mock_dl.return_value = [MagicMock(type="text", text='{"status":"success"}')]
+            result = await call_tool("download_paper", {"paper_id": "123"})
+            mock_dl.assert_awaited_once()
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_list_papers_dispatches(self):
+        with patch("arxiv_mcp_server.server.handle_list_papers", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_lp:
+            mock_lp.return_value = [MagicMock(type="text", text='{"total_papers":0}')]
+            result = await call_tool("list_papers", {})
+            mock_lp.assert_awaited_once()
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_read_paper_dispatches(self):
+        with patch("arxiv_mcp_server.server.handle_read_paper", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_rp:
+            mock_rp.return_value = [MagicMock(type="text", text='{"paper_id":"123"}')]
+            result = await call_tool("read_paper", {"paper_id": "123"})
+            mock_rp.assert_awaited_once()
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_call_tool_exception_wrapped(self):
+        with patch("arxiv_mcp_server.server.handle_search", new_callable=AsyncMock) as mock_s:
+            mock_s.side_effect = Exception("boom")
+            result = await call_tool("search_papers", {"query": "x"})
+            assert len(result) == 1
+            assert "Error:" in result[0].text or "boom" in result[0].text
+
+
+class TestServerPromptHandlers:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_handle_list_prompts(self):
+        prompts = await handle_list_prompts()
+        assert isinstance(prompts, list)
+        names = [p.name for p in prompts]
+        assert "deep-paper-analysis" in names
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_handle_get_prompt_valid(self):
+        result = await handle_get_prompt("deep-paper-analysis", {"paper_id": "2401.00123"})
+        assert "2401.00123" in result.messages[0].content.text
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_handle_get_prompt_invalid(self):
+        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Prompt not found"):
+            await handle_get_prompt("nope", {})
+
+
+class TestServerResourceHandlers:
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_handle_list_resources_empty(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        result = await handle_list_resources()
+        assert result == []
+
+    @pytest.mark.asyncio
+    async def test_handle_read_resource(self, tmp_path, mocker):
+        (tmp_path / "test.md").write_text("# Paper\nContent", encoding="utf-8")
+        mock_settings = mocker.patch("arxiv_mcp_server.resources.papers.settings")
+        mock_settings.STORAGE_PATH = tmp_path
+        result = await handle_read_resource("arxiv://test")
+        items = list(result)
+        assert len(items) == 1
+        assert items[0].content == "# Paper\nContent"
+
+
+class TestServerInstance:
+    def test_server_name(self):
+        assert server.name == "arxiv-mcp-server"
+
+    def test_server_version(self):
+        assert server.version == "0.2.11"
+
+    def test_server_instructions(self):
+        assert server.instructions is not None
+        assert "arXiv" in server.instructions or "ArXiv" in server.instructions
